summary: The US-Iran ceasefire announced Tuesday night has paused a five-week conflict that had closed the Strait of Hormuz and sent equity prices reeling. WTI crude tumbled roughly 18% to under $93 on the news, relieving the energy shock that had been suppressing stocks and fueling inflation fears.
tweet: The Iran ceasefire crushed oil prices and lifted stocks, but tariff-driven goods inflation and a jittery DXY keep sentiment stuck in neutral across assets.
